records.ety: [AS. emtig, æmtig, æmetig, fr. æmta, æmetta, quiet, leisure, rest; of uncertain origin; cf. G. emsig busy.]

defs: Containing nothing; not holding or having anything within; void of contents or appropriate contents; not filled; -- said of an inclosure, or a container, as a box, room, house, etc.; as, an empty chest, room, purse, or pitcher; an empty stomach; empty shackles.
Free; clear; devoid; -- often with of.
quotes: I shall find you empty of that fault.
When ye go ye shall not go empty.

defs: Destitute of effect, sincerity, or sense; -- said of language; as, empty words, or threats.
Unable to satisfy; unsatisfactory; hollow; vain; -- said of pleasure, the world, etc.
quotes: Pleas'd in the silent shade with empty praise.

defs: Producing nothing; unfruitful; -- said of a plant or tree; as, an empty vine.
quotes: Seven empty ears blasted with the east wind.

defs: Destitute of, or lacking, sense, knowledge, or courtesy; as, empty brains; an empty coxcomb.
quotes: That in civility thou seem'st so empty.

defs: Destitute of reality, or real existence; unsubstantial; as, empty dreams.
notes: ☞ Empty is used as the first element in a compound; as, empty-handed, having nothing in the hands, destitute; empty-headed, having few ideas; empty-hearted, destitute of feeling.

defs: An empty box, crate, cask, etc.; -- used in commerce, esp. in transportation of freight; as, “special rates for empties.”

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Emptied (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Emptying.]

defs: To deprive of the contents; to exhaust; to make void or destitute; to make vacant; to pour out; to discharge; as, to empty a vessel; to empty a well or a cistern.
quotes: The clouds . . . empty themselves upon the earth.

defs: To discharge itself; as, a river empties into the ocean.
